message:
beads: close bd-212m.5.2 mouse drag support - verified parity

Evaluation complete. Rust Bubbletea has full mouse drag parity with Go:
- Drag events: crossterm Drag(_) maps to Motion action with button preserved
- All 12 mouse buttons supported (None, Left, Middle, Right, Wheel*, etc.)
- All modifiers (Shift, Alt, Ctrl) supported
- Deprecated Go Type field intentionally not implemented

No code changes needed - existing implementation is complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-212m.4.3","title":"Huh: fix KeyMap file_picker initializer compile error","description":"Benchmark E2E compile currently fails because KeyMap initializer does not include file_picker in at least one cfg path. This blocks benchmark_e2e::compilation_tests::test_benchmarks_compile during cargo test -p charmed_conformance.

Scope:
- Find all KeyMap initializers and feature-gated cfgs in crates/huh/src/lib.rs (and any tests/benches).
- Ensure file_picker: FilePickerKeyMap::default() is set everywhere a KeyMap is constructed.
- Add regression coverage (unit + compile in bench path) and structured logs.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- cargo check -p huh --all-targets passes (no missing field errors).
- cargo test -p charmed_conformance -- --nocapture passes benchmark_e2e::compilation_tests::test_benchmarks_compile.
- New unit test (e.g., test_keymap_includes_file_picker) asserts default KeyMap includes file_picker bindings.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated with latest run details.","notes":"Checked crates/huh/src/lib.rs: KeyMap::new includes file_picker; no other KeyMap struct literals present. `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` and benchmark_e2e::compilation_tests::test_benchmarks_compile now pass (CARGO_HOME=target/mistyotter_cargo_home6 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/mistyotter_benchfix), so original missing-field error is no longer reproducible. Likely fixed by recent edits; closing as resolved.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:21:51.845194607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T02:05:30.273493830Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T02:05:30.273288794Z","close_reason":"No longer reproducible; KeyMap::new includes file_picker and bench compile passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:21:51.845194607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.5","title":"Bubbletea parity gaps","description":"Close Bubbletea parity gaps: custom I/O event injection and mouse drag support evaluation.

Scope:
- Implement custom I/O event injection path for non-standard inputs (per program.rs note).
- Evaluate mouse drag support vs Go and decide implementation plan.
- Add unit + conformance tests to ensure expected message flow and mouse events.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Custom I/O injection works and is covered by tests/fixtures.
- Mouse drag support evaluated with documented decision (implemented or explicitly excluded) and FEATURE_PARITY.md updated.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/input.rs (if present), tests/conformance/crates/bubbletea/mod.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:32.471902669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:27:24.610035289Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.5","depends_on_id":"bd-212m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:32.471902669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.5.1","title":"Bubbletea: implement custom I/O event injection","description":"Implement custom I/O event injection path in Bubbletea to match Go behavior.

Scope:
- Use program.rs note at line ~432: custom I/O assumes events injected via other means.
- Design a public API for injecting input events/messages when Program is configured with custom input/output streams.
- Ensure injection integrates with the event loop and preserves ordering with crossterm events.
- Add unit tests for injected events and conformance fixtures if needed.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- New API allows injecting events/messages without crossterm (custom I/O mode).
- Unit tests cover injection ordering, shutdown, and error handling.
- Conformance tests updated to include a custom I/O injection fixture (if Go supports it).
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to remove Bubbletea custom I/O gap.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (event loop), crates/bubbletea/src/cmd.rs (if relevant), tests/conformance/crates/bubbletea/mod.rs. Maintain async + sync parity.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:39.053570122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:27:32.293668078Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:39.053570122Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-212m.5.2","title":"Bubbletea: evaluate mouse drag support","description":"Evaluate and optionally implement mouse drag support to match Go Bubbletea.

Scope:
- Compare Rust mouse event parsing with Go (drag/selection events).
- Identify gaps in crossterm mouse event support and how Bubbletea maps them to messages.
- Decide: implement drag event synthesis or document limitation.
- Add tests or documentation updates accordingly.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Decision documented: implement drag events or explicitly exclude with rationale.
- If implemented: unit tests + conformance fixtures for drag sequences.
- README limitations updated if drag remains limited.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/input.rs (mouse parsing), crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (event loop), README.md limitations section.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:46.941228385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:27:41.359235510Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:46.941228385Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-212m.6","title":"Bubbles parity audit & fixture coverage","description":"Audit Bubbles component parity and fixture coverage. Although current conformance passes, we need to ensure coverage is comprehensive and that any skip stubs are removed or replaced with real tests.

Scope:
- Component-by-component parity audits (stopwatch, timer, list, table, paginator, help, viewport, cursor, keybinding, filepicker, textinput).
- Expand fixtures and unit tests to cover edge cases and Go behavior.
- Remove dead SKIPPED branches once fixtures are comprehensive.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Each component audit produces either (a) confirmation of parity with evidence or (b) a concrete fix + tests.
- Fixtures updated to cover edge cases and avoid skipped paths.
- No SKIPPED branches remain in tests/conformance/crates/bubbles/mod.rs unless explicitly justified.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbles/src/*, tests/conformance/crates/bubbles/mod.rs, tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/bubbles.json. Use legacy_bubbles as reference.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:54.737129295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:27:49.603586771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-212m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:54.737129295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.6.1","title":"Bubbles: stopwatch parity audit","description":"Audit Stopwatch component parity vs Go.

Scope:
- Compare Rust stopwatch behavior to legacy_bubbles stopwatch (start/stop/reset/tick).
- Expand fixtures to cover timing precision, reset semantics, and formatting.
- Add unit tests for tick progression and formatting output.
- Fix any discrepancies uncovered.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Conformance fixtures for stopwatch cover start/stop/reset and formatting edge cases.
- Unit tests validate elapsed time formatting and state transitions.
- Any discovered discrepancy is fixed and recorded in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbles/src/stopwatch.rs, tests/conformance/crates/bubbles/mod.rs (stopwatch fixtures), tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/bubbles.json.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:50:06.501803827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:37:35.219166711Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:37:35.218796624Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:50:06.501803827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.6.10","title":"Bubbles: filepicker parity audit","description":"Audit FilePicker component parity vs Go.

Scope:
- Compare directory navigation, filtering by extension, hidden files, and sorting.
- Expand fixtures for edge cases: empty dirs, permission errors, root navigation.
- Add unit tests for path formatting and selection behavior.
- Fix any discrepancies uncovered.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Conformance fixtures cover filepicker navigation and filtering edge cases.
- Unit tests validate selection and display formatting.
- Any parity gaps fixed and documented.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbles/src/filepicker.rs, tests/conformance/crates/bubbles/mod.rs (filepicker fixtures).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:51:25.273281874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:09:38.774287813Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:09:38.774265521Z","close_reason":"FilePicker: clamp selection after dir read/resize; selection respects allowed_types/dir_allowed; prevent stale path in did_select_*; update disabled styling; add tests; ran fmt/check/clippy + filepicker tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.6.10","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:51:25.273281874Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
